plainEnglish: If your content imposes a time limit—a session timeout, an expiring form, a countdown—let users turn it off, adjust it, or extend it (with a warning and at least 20 seconds to respond). People who read slowly or use assistive tech need more time. A few real-time exceptions like auctions apply.
whyItMatters: Users with cognitive disabilities, low vision, or motor impairments who need more time lose their work or get logged out when a time limit can't be extended.
quickCheck: Identify every time limit (session timeout, redirect, countdown) and confirm users can turn it off, extend it, or are warned with 20+ seconds to act; check for <meta http-equiv="refresh"> (axe meta-refresh).
commonMistakes: Using <meta http-equiv="refresh"> to auto-redirect or reload on a timer (axe meta-refresh).
Session timeouts that log users out with no warning or way to extend.
Forms that silently expire and discard input after a fixed time.
Auto-advancing carousels or quizzes with a countdown that can't be paused or extended.

codeExamples.label: Warn before timeout and let the user extend
Forced timed redirect with no user control
codeExamples.code:
<!-- Redirects after 30s with no way to stop or extend it -->
<meta http-equiv="refresh" content="30; url=/timed-out" />
